deep_dive.title: Toward Production-Ready Federated Learning in Healthcare: Privacy, Orchestration, and Governance in MLOps
deep_dive.abstract: Healthcare organizations often cannot freely centralize patient data because medical records are sensitive, regulated, and institutionally controlled. Federated learning offers a practical alternative by allowing hospitals and clinics to train a shared model while keeping raw data local. However, federated learning is not automatically production-ready or private by default. Model updates can still leak information, and decentralized training introduces operational challenges in deployment, monitoring, rollback, debugging, and governance. This paper examines how MLOps practices and the emerging idea of Federated Learning Operations (FLOps) can make federated healthcare machine learning systems scalable, reliable, and trustworthy. It answers three research questions: how containerization and orchestration support federated deployment, how privacy-preserving mechanisms affect trade-offs among privacy, utility, scalability, and operational complexity, and which post-deployment practices are most important for long-term governance. The central argument is that federated healthcare ML requires more than privacy-preserving algorithms. It needs an integrated MLOps architecture that combines reproducible deployment, secure orchestration, model versioning, audit logging, drift monitoring, heterogeneity management, and clear governance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: production-ready federated learning privacy and governance in healthcare
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 医疗领域生产级联邦学习的隐私、编排与治理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 医疗领域生产级联邦学习的隐私、编排与治理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 医疗领域生产级联邦学习的隐私、编排与治理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Examines MLOps and FLOps to make federated learning in healthcare scalable, reliable, and privacy-aware, addressing operational challenges.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文探讨医疗领域联邦学习在生产环境中面临的隐私、编排和治理挑战。提出借鉴MLOps实践和联邦学习运营(FLOps)理念，使分布式训练系统可扩展、可靠并符合隐私法规。重点关注模型更新泄露信息问题以及部署、监控等运营难题。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文探讨医疗领域联邦学习在生产环境中面临的隐私、编排和治理挑战。提出借鉴MLOps实践和联邦学习运营(FLOps)理念，使分布式训练系统可扩展、可靠并符合隐私法规。重点关注模型更新泄露信息问题以及部署、监控等运营难题。

deep_dive.title: Privacy-Aware Collaborative and Distributed Bayesian Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: We propose a collaborative meta-learning framework for distributed Bayesian optimization matching centralized performance without raw-data exchange. We show gradient sharing leaks client observations, with leakage worsening as the search converges and queries concentrate near the optimum. We evaluate a differentially private defense and characterize its privacy-utility trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分布式贝叶斯优化中的隐私保护
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分布式贝叶斯优化中的隐私保护
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分布式贝叶斯优化中的隐私保护
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a privacy-aware distributed Bayesian optimization framework using differential privacy to protect gradient sharing, analyzing the privacy-utility trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对分布式贝叶斯优化中的隐私泄露问题，提出了一种协作式元学习框架，无需交换原始数据即可达到集中式性能。研究发现梯度共享会泄露客户端观测信息，且随搜索收敛泄露加剧。论文评估了差分隐私防御机制，并刻画了隐私-效用权衡。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对分布式贝叶斯优化中的隐私泄露问题，提出了一种协作式元学习框架，无需交换原始数据即可达到集中式性能。研究发现梯度共享会泄露客户端观测信息，且随搜索收敛泄露加剧。论文评估了差分隐私防御机制，并刻画了隐私-效用权衡。

deep_dive.title: PromptGraph: Graph-Guided Prompt Sanitization for Balancing Privacy and Utility in LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) services introduce a fundamental privacy challenge. Sensitive information may be inferred not only from explicit identifiers, such as names or phone numbers, but also from contextual associations among otherwise innocuous spans. Existing sanitizers typically assign privacy or utility signals to individual spans without explicitly modeling pairwise relationships among them. In this paper, we propose PromptGraph, a graph-guided prompt-sanitization approach for privacy-preserving LLM inference. PromptGraph estimates privacy leakage at the span level and utility-relevant contextual dependencies between pairs of spans. It represents each prompt as an attributed graph, in which nodes carry span-level privacy scores and edges encode contextual dependencies needed to preserve utility. The sanitization objective selects a protected span set that maximizes privacy gain while penalizing the loss of contextual dependencies. This formulation explicitly balances privacy and utility when contextual evidence is hidden. Protected spans are sanitized locally, and returned placeholders are restored only after passing local consistency checks. We conduct extensive experiments showing that PromptGraph achieves a more favorable balance between privacy and utility than prompt-privacy baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: privacy-preserving LLM inference via prompt sanitization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过提示清洗实现隐私保护的LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过提示清洗实现隐私保护的LLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过提示清洗实现隐私保护的LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PromptGraph, a graph-guided prompt sanitization that models span-level privacy and contextual dependencies, balancing privacy and utility; directly relevant to privacy in distributed ML.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对LLM推理中的隐私泄露问题，提出PromptGraph方法。该方法将提示建模为属性图，节点携带隐私信号，边反映上下文依赖，从而实现细粒度的隐私清洗。实验表明，PromptGraph在保护隐私的同时有效保留了语义实用性，直接相关于分布式机器学习中的隐私保护。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对LLM推理中的隐私泄露问题，提出PromptGraph方法。该方法将提示建模为属性图，节点携带隐私信号，边反映上下文依赖，从而实现细粒度的隐私清洗。实验表明，PromptGraph在保护隐私的同时有效保留了语义实用性，直接相关于分布式机器学习中的隐私保护。

quick_skim.title: Differentially Private Consistent Release of Counting Queries
quick_skim.abstract: We study the problem of releasing counting-query outputs through a stochastic mechanism that is both consistent and \((ε,δ)\)-differentially private. Consistency requires the released value to lie within the feasible range of the query, while utility is measured by the worst-case probability of error. We first derive a closed-form expression for the minimum achievable error probability and obtain an explicit optimal mechanism. By exploiting the active differential privacy constraints satisfied by this mechanism, we then characterize the entire class of optimal mechanisms via a propagation argument, identifying the structural properties shared by all optimizers.   We next extend the framework to the setting in which the privacy mechanism is cascaded with an arbitrary fixed stochastic transformation representing a predetermined portion of the communication medium between the source and the destination. We first establish necessary and sufficient conditions under which this partial fixation of the medium incurs no loss in utility. We then derive upper and lower bounds on the optimal achievable performance based on convex mixing and spectral perturbation. Finally, we apply the theory to (M)-ary phase-shift keying (PSK) transmission over an additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) channel and show that uncoded transmission is effectively optimal in the high-privacy regime.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: differential privacy for counting queries, applicable to distributed machine learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 计数查询的差分隐私机制，可用于分布式机器学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 计数查询的差分隐私机制，可用于分布式机器学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 计数查询的差分隐私机制，可用于分布式机器学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies differentially private release of counting queries with consistency, deriving optimal mechanisms and their structural properties, useful for privacy-preserving distributed learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究了计数查询的差分隐私一致性发布问题，要求发布值在查询可行范围内且满足差分隐私。作者导出了最小误差概率的闭式解和显式最优机制，并通过传播参数刻画了全部最优机制的结构。该工作为在分布式机器学习等场景下实现高精度、可证明的隐私保护查询发布提供了理论基础和最优设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文研究了计数查询的差分隐私一致性发布问题，要求发布值在查询可行范围内且满足差分隐私。作者导出了最小误差概率的闭式解和显式最优机制，并通过传播参数刻画了全部最优机制的结构。该工作为在分布式机器学习等场景下实现高精度、可证明的隐私保护查询发布提供了理论基础和最优设计。
